content: Just as second-language vocabulary learning uses deliberate reflective writing (the educational linguistic sense of reflexion) helps learners correct logical errors by stepping outside their own reasoning to refine output, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can borrow this structured, language-focused reflexion practice to improve their output accuracy. Structured reflexion prompts formatted like high school writing workshop writing revision exercises train LLMs to explicitly spot inconsistent reasoning gaps across multiple solving perspectives, just as student writers learn to catch logical flaws in their own arguments.
